notes: Hooks under harness/hooks/ are out of scope (Claude executes them in its own env). 11 .sh files in scope; 11 .ps1 ports to produce.

sprints.title: Foundation libs: utils, git, invoke
sprints.rationale: Pure helper modules — no dependents inside the harness yet. Validating these first lets every later sprint dot-source them with confidence.
acceptanceCriteria: All three .ps1 files exist beside their .sh counterparts.
Each parses cleanly via [Parser]::ParseFile with zero errors.
Each begins with #Requires -Version 7.0, Set-StrictMode -Version Latest, $ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'.

utils.ps1 exposes the same logical helpers used by .sh (logging, JSON read/write, status writes, cost append, Invoke-OrFail wrapper). Public function names use PowerShell Verb-Noun convention but cover every behavior the .sh provides.
git.ps1 wraps git/gh calls with the same observable side effects (branch, tag, commit message format 'harness(sprint-NN): ... [C-ID]', tag names 'harness/sprint-NN/pass' and 'harness/plan').
invoke.ps1 runs the claude CLI with the same flags the .sh uses, captures cost/usage to harness-state/cost-log.json with the same schema.
No POSIX tool names (jq, grep, sed, awk, bash, wsl) appear as bare commands in any of the three files (string literals & comments OK).
Dot-source pattern: `. (Join-Path $PSScriptRoot 'utils.ps1')` works from sibling scripts (verifiable by a small inline test the generator runs).

sprints.title: Agent libs: planner, generator, evaluator, contract
sprints.rationale: These wrap claude invocations for each agent role. They depend on utils/git/invoke from Sprint 01.
acceptanceCriteria: All four .ps1 files exist and parse.
Each dot-sources utils.ps1 (and others as needed) using $PSScriptRoot.

planner.ps1 writes harness-state/product-spec.md and harness-state/sprint-plan.json — same paths, same JSON shape (sprints[].id, .title, .deliverables, .dependencies, .acceptanceCriteria, .regressionConcerns).
generator.ps1 writes harness-state/sprints/sprint-NN/generator-log.md and updates handoff.json identically to the .sh version.
evaluator.ps1 writes harness-state/sprints/sprint-NN/eval-report.json with the same schema (status, blockers, regressionResults, summary).
contract.ps1 implements the propose↔review loop with maxContractRounds (read from config.json), writing sprint-contract.json with the same fields the .sh produces.
Same agent prompts are loaded from harness/prompts/* — these prompt files are reused unchanged.
No bare POSIX tool invocations.

sprints.title: Top-level orchestrator
sprints.rationale: orchestrate.sh is the longest and most complex script (521 lines). It glues everything together. Doing it after Sprint 01+02 means the generator can lean on those .ps1 helpers exactly the way the .sh leans on its libs.
acceptanceCriteria: harness/orchestrate.ps1 exists, parses cleanly.
Dot-sources harness/lib/*.ps1 from $PSScriptRoot, mirroring the .sh sourcing layout.

Same positional arg + same flags as orchestrate.sh: `pwsh -NoProfile -File harness/orchestrate.ps1 "<userPrompt>" [--max-cost N] [--project-type T] [--resume]` (extract the exact flag list from orchestrate.sh and match it 1:1).
Honors costCapPerSprint and totalCostCap from harness-state/config.json with same enforcement semantics.
Produces the same git history shape: branches `harness/sprint-NN`, tags `harness/plan`, `harness/sprint-NN/pass`, attempt tags on failure.
Same commit messages: 'harness(sprint-NN): ...', 'harness(eval): sprint-NN result', 'harness(plan): product spec and sprint plan', 'harness(contract): sprint-NN agreed'.
On success, exits 0 after either creating a PR (if `gh` available) or writing harness-state/pr-body.md.
--help prints a usage summary mirroring orchestrate.sh's `usage()` text and exits 0.
No bare POSIX tool invocations.

sprints.title: Test runners: run-all, smoke-test, meta-test
sprints.rationale: Shipped last because they exercise everything above. Independent of the orchestrator at runtime — they call bats / claude / git directly.
acceptanceCriteria: All three .ps1 files exist and parse.

tests/run-all.ps1 accepts the same `--layer N` / `--all` flags as run-all.sh, calls bats for Layer 1 (bats is acceptable because users run tests in environments where bats-core is installable; the .sh version does the same), invokes the .ps1 layer2/layer3 runners.

tests/layer2/smoke-test.ps1 performs the same end-to-end smoke flow as smoke-test.sh: invokes orchestrate.ps1 with a fixed mini-prompt, asserts harness-state/ contents, exits 0/non-zero matching the .sh.
tests/layer3/meta-test.ps1 reproduces the meta self-test: harness builds its own test, asserts artifacts.
All three exit 0 on `--help` and print usage.
No bare POSIX tool invocations.
